compiled_state.title: Review autonomous resolution loop does not escalate to higher-tier reviewer after repeated failures
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Bug Description

During sprint execution of epic 83fc-81fb batch 5, the autonomous resolution loop ran 5 passes with the standard-tier reviewer (dso:code-reviewer-standard, sonnet) and exhausted max_resolution_attempts without escalating to a higher-tier reviewer (opus). The REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md specifies that the review-complexity-classifier.sh supports model upgrades for difficult diffs (300+ lines → opus), but the autonomous resolution loop does not re-classify or upgrade the reviewer tier between passes.

## Root Cause

The orchestrator re-dispatched the same standard-tier reviewer (sonnet) for all 5 resolution passes. It should have escalated to opus-tier review (dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch or at minimum a standard-tier with opus model override) after 2-3 failed passes, following the same escalation pattern used elsewhere in the sprint (e.g., sub-agent model escalation on failure in Phase 6 Step 0).

## Expected Behavior

After N failed review passes at a given tier (e.g., 2-3 passes at standard/sonnet), the resolution loop should:
1. Re-classify the diff at the next tier up (standard → deep, or sonnet → opus model override)
2. Dispatch the higher-tier reviewer for the remaining resolution attempts
3. The higher-tier reviewer may resolve findings that the lower-tier reviewer was unable to

## Observed Behavior

All 5 passes used dso:code-reviewer-standard (sonnet). The reviewer repeatedly flagged the same by-design architectural decisions (template without consumer in current batch, deferred test-cases.yaml entries) without escalation to an opus reviewer that might have recognized the multi-batch TDD pattern.

## Affected Files

- REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md (autonomous resolution loop section)
- Sprint SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 7 (review dispatch)

## Impact

Low — workaround is manual user escalation after max attempts. But the autonomous resolution loop loses 2-3 passes worth of context by not escalating early.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
